notes: Heavy geopolitics/Iran conflict noise across all 3 scrapes. Filtered @DeItaone, @financialjuice, @unusual_whales political/conflict content. Filtered old CNBC clips (2022-2024), crypto shills, film promos, personal motivation posts.

findings.summary: 39K new users to graphed.com from organic only: 70 tweets/week, 1 LinkedIn post/day, 1 podcast/week, 1 newsletter, 1 YouTube. Paid ads only 1% remarketing.
findings.extracted_method: High-volume multi-channel organic distribution: 70 tweets/week + daily LinkedIn + weekly podcast + newsletter + YouTube. No paid acquisition. Remarketing only. The volume is the strategy, not clever tactics.
findings.implementation_notes: We already have content queue with 283+ posts. The gap is POSTING FREQUENCY. 70 tweets/week = 10/day. We need to actually post what we have, not build more.

findings.summary: GTM Engineer is the most desirable hire in 2026. 40+ DMs in 7 days asking how to hire one. Friend negotiated massive salary jump between startups.
findings.extracted_method: Position as GTM engineer for hire or create a Gumroad product: 'GTM Engineering Playbook'. The demand signal is real (40+ DMs = validated interest). Could also create a Fiverr/Upwork gig offering GTM engineering as a service.
findings.implementation_notes: We have cold email + automation skills that map to GTM engineering. Package existing knowledge into a $49-99 digital product or freelance gig listing.

findings.summary: Cold email framing: stop describing service, start describing outcome. 'We do SEO' becomes '9 calls/month by being recommended on ChatGPT'. 'We manage HubSpot' becomes '$50K from dead leads in 60 days or you don't pay'.
findings.extracted_method: Reframe all cold email copy from service-description to outcome-description. Every service pitch should answer 'what specific measurable result do they get?' with a number and timeframe. Add risk reversal ('or you don't pay').
findings.implementation_notes: Apply immediately to all existing cold email templates in EMAIL/sequences/. 1 hour to rewrite. Also update Fiverr/Upwork gig descriptions.

findings.summary: Sell new revenue, not cost savings in cold email. Cost savings requires trust (believe, evaluate, care, want to change). New revenue only requires curiosity. Curiosity gets replies.
findings.extracted_method: Every cold email should pitch NEW REVENUE opportunity, not cost reduction. The psychological barrier to 'save money' is trust (4 steps). The barrier to 'make more money' is curiosity (1 step). Reframe all pitches around revenue generation.
findings.implementation_notes: Audit all email templates. Replace any 'save X' framing with 'earn X' framing. Applies to both our outbound and our product copy.

findings.summary: Sold digital products daily for 14 months. Biggest lesson: only 3 tweet types generate sales. Product itself isn't the problem, tweet structure is. Lost $6,200 before figuring this out.
findings.extracted_method: There are only 3 tweet structures that convert to digital product sales. The thread teases but doesn't reveal them (funnel to Telegram). Method: study our own analytics for which tweet formats drive clicks to Gumroad/Whop links. Likely: problem-agitate-solution, result-then-method, social-proof-then-CTA.
findings.implementation_notes: Account runs a Telegram funnel with 'free guides' CTAs on every thread. Earnings claim of $6,200 loss is unverified. But the insight about tweet structure mattering more than product quality is valid. Test 3 tweet formats for our Gumroad products.

findings.summary: Analyzed digital product accounts across Gumroad, Whop, Stan Store. 90% fighting over scraps. Real money in accounts selling quietly (not posting screenshots). Found patterns in what actually sells vs what gets engagement.
findings.extracted_method: Study quiet sellers on Gumroad/Whop/Stan Store rather than flashy screenshot posters. The accounts that post revenue screenshots are selling to wannabe-sellers (meta-market). The real product-market fit is in niches where people buy quietly. Cross-reference Gumroad trending with low-social-media-presence sellers.
findings.implementation_notes: We have 13 Gumroad products. Run a competitive scan of Gumroad trending page and identify sellers with high sales but low Twitter following. Their niches and pricing are the signal.

findings.summary: Warning about using GoMarble MCP for Claude Code bulk uploads. They used a marketing API key in dev mode. Cody only recommends personal-use dev mode API keys for bulk operations.
findings.extracted_method: Claude Code can bulk upload to marketing platforms using personal dev-mode API keys. GoMarble MCP exists for this but has compliance risks. Safer approach: generate personal API keys in dev mode for platforms, use Claude Code scripts to bulk upload content/ads.
findings.implementation_notes: Relevant for our content distribution pipeline. Could use Claude Code + API keys to bulk schedule/upload to platforms we use. Investigate GoMarble MCP but use personal API keys only.

findings.summary: Make $10k/month on X as Whop affiliate for AI UGC info products. AI UGC tweets go super viral. Affiliate for existing UGC products rather than creating your own.
findings.extracted_method: Become a Whop affiliate for AI UGC products. Post viral 'how to make realistic UGC with Sora 2' style tweets. These get massive organic reach. Affiliate link in bio/replies. No product creation needed, just content + affiliate link.
findings.implementation_notes: $10k/month claim is unverified and likely inflated. But the tactic of affiliating for AI tool/UGC products via viral tweets is sound. We already have affiliate infrastructure. Sign up for Whop affiliate programs for top AI UGC courses.

findings.summary: In-depth thread on how to start a branded YouTube channel the right way. Covers niche finding, ideation methodology. 143 likes, 9.3K views with authentic engagement ratio.
findings.extracted_method: Branded YouTube channel playbook: 1) Study existing successful formats in target niche, 2) Identify repeating structures in their most popular videos, 3) Reverse-engineer the pattern, 4) Apply to your niche with unique angle. Patterns are not accidental - they're proven frameworks.
findings.implementation_notes: Relevant for our faceless YouTube channel plans. Cross-reference with @saimagnate's post about studying YT formats. The method is competitor reverse-engineering, not original content creation.

findings.summary: Getting $11 RPM vs industry average $2 RPM. Claims article explains how. Higher RPM niches exist if you target right audience demographics.
findings.extracted_method: RPM optimization: $11 vs $2 per 1000 views is a 5.5x multiplier. The difference is niche selection and audience demographics. Finance, B2B, and tech niches command higher ad rates. Target content at audiences advertisers pay premium for (25-54 professionals, US/UK/AU).
findings.implementation_notes: Directly applicable to our YouTube and content strategy. Instead of chasing views in low-RPM niches, create content that attracts high-RPM audiences. Our solopreneur/AI tools niche is already high-RPM. Double down on it.
